description:
You are a build-engineer who has been asked to bump an internal release version and embed a new
    checksum across two configuration files that live in the same folder.  
    Everything you need is already on disk.

    1. Location of the folder to work in (already present):  
       /home/user/build_configs

    2. Files that exist right now:

       a. /home/user/build_configs/app.yaml  
          Current content:
              name: sample-app
              version: "1.2.3"

       b. /home/user/build_configs/release.toml  
          Current content:
              [build]
              number = 102

              [metadata]
              checksum = ""

    3. What you must change (only these four edits, nothing else):

       a. In app.yaml  
          • Change the value of key “version” to the string "1.3.0"  
          • Add a new sibling key directly under the version line named “artifact_sha”  
            with the value "abc123def456" (quotes are required).

       b. In release.toml  
          • Change the value of build.number from 102 to 103  
          • Change the value of metadata.checksum from an empty string to  
            "abc123def456" (quotes are required).

    4. Create a plaintext log file called /home/user/build_configs/build_update.log
       that documents the four edits in the EXACT five-line format shown below
       (newline after every line, no extra spaces, no extra blank lines).
       This file is what the automated tests will read!

           UPDATE_LOG_START
           app.yaml: version -> 1.3.0
           app.yaml: artifact_sha -> abc123def456
           release.toml: build.number -> 103
           release.toml: checksum -> abc123def456
           UPDATE_LOG_END

       The log must match those six lines byte-for-byte, including the two
       literal UPDATE_LOG_* markers and the newline following UPDATE_LOG_END.

    5. Accomplish everything with standard CLI tooling available in a typical
       Debian/Ubuntu environment (no root privileges).  A single well-crafted
       shell command is sufficient, but you may take more steps if you wish.
       When you are done, the content of the two configuration files and the
       build_update.log must match the specification above exactly.
truth:

    Files:

           name: sample-app
           version: "1.2.3"

        2. /home/user/build_configs/release.toml   (mode 644)
           [build]
           number = 102

           [metadata]
           checksum = ""

    Required final state after the agent finishes:

        1. /home/user/build_configs/app.yaml   (mode 644)
           name: sample-app
           version: "1.3.0"
           artifact_sha: "abc123def456"

        2. /home/user/build_configs/release.toml   (mode 644)
           [build]
           number = 103

           [metadata]
           checksum = "abc123def456"

        3. /home/user/build_configs/build_update.log   (mode 644)
           UPDATE_LOG_START
           app.yaml: version -> 1.3.0
           app.yaml: artifact_sha -> abc123def456
           release.toml: build.number -> 103
           release.toml: checksum -> abc123def456
           UPDATE_LOG_END
